title: Boat Hire in Cannes France: 2026 Costs, Routes and Tips
html:
Hiring a boat in Cannes France offers a stylish gateway to the Bay of Cannes and the Lérins Islands, with prices for a half-day small motorboat starting around 250 EUR through to 2,500 EUR for a crewed yacht. The best place to begin is at the Marché de la location de bateaux, where you can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cannes France?

A small motorboat for half a day typically costs between 250 and 400 EUR, while a full-day catamaran ranges from 800 to 1,500 EUR. Crewed luxury yachts of 20 metres or more can exceed 3,000 EUR per day. Prices vary by season and boat size, and it is wise to check day charters in Cannes France for current availability.

  • Shared tour: from 60 EUR per person for a three-hour trip
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 guests): from 250 EUR
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 12 guests): from 800 EUR
  • Large crewed motor yacht (full-day, 10+ guests): from 2,500 EUR

Where to set off in Cannes France

The main departure points are the four marinas of Cannes: Port Pierre Canto east of the Croisette, Vieux Port in the centre, Port du Mouré Rouge in La Bocca, and the smaller Port Pointe Croisette by the Palm Beach casino. From any of these you can reach the nearby Île Sainte-Marguerite in about 20 minutes (2.5 nautical miles) or motor to the Porquerolles islands in the Hyères archipelago for a full-day voyage (around 15 nautical miles). Many boaters also anchor off the island of Saint-Honorat, home to the Lérins Abbey, or take a longer cruise to the beach clubs of La Napoule and Théoule-sur-Mer.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lets you choose from a range of vessels. Here are the most popular types for a trip in Cannes:

  1. Small speedboat (e.g. Axopar 28), ideal for an active day exploring coves; seats up to 6; from 350 EUR per half day. Visit the Limpopo charter page for details of this model.
  2. Sports cruiser (e.g. Fairline 38), comfortable for 8 guests with a cabin; from 600 EUR per half day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), stable and spacious for up to 12; from 800 EUR full day.
  4. Flybridge motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 45), stylish, with aft deck; 8 guests; from 1,200 EUR full day.
  5. Luxury yacht (e.g. Maiora 26), crewed, 12+ guests, 2,500 EUR full day; pictured below.

What is the best season to visit Cannes France for boating?

The high season for boating in Cannes runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive months. The Mistral wind can blow strongly from the north-west in spring and autumn, but the summer months generally offer calm conditions. The sea breeze, locally called the vent marin, picks up in the afternoon. For quieter anchorages, consider May and mid-September.

Book your rental at least two weeks ahead in July and August; many popular boats are taken by returning clients. A morning departure avoids the strongest afternoon chop and gives you prime spots at the Île Sainte-Marguerite.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cannes France?

In France, you generally need a boating licence for motorboats over 6 metres or with more than 10.5 kW of power, but the operator will confirm the exact requirements for your chosen vessel. Many charter companies offer licence-free rentals for smaller boats (under 6 metres or with lower power), often after a short briefing. Larger motor yachts and catamarans will require a valid ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or a French permis bateau. Always check the specific boat listing for licence details.

At a glance

  • Price range: 250 EUR (half-day small motorboat) to 3,000+ EUR (full-day luxury yacht)
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical half-day trip: 4 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12+ depending on boat type
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Cannes France

Can I bring my own food and drink?

Yes, most self-drive charters allow you to bring a picnic and drinks, though crewed yachts often include catering.

How many people can fit on a small motorboat?

Small speedboats typically carry between 4 and 6 passengers, including the skipper if you hire one.

What is the cancellation policy?

Cancellation terms vary by listing; many offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure.

Is Cannes safe to anchor off the beaches?

Yes, but avoid swimming lanes and keep clear of the buoyed protected zones near the Lérins Islands.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cannes France or list your own. Whether you are a seasoned boater or a first-time visitor, browse the range of vessels available through the BoatHire24 marketplace. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cannes France and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
